meta.name: Lime-Chili Shrimp
meta.subtitle: Fast skillet shrimp with bright lime, garlic, and adjustable chili heat

source.name: SSA Canonical Catalog
notes: Designed for Citrus-Chili cuisine overlay: bright citrus + clean chili warmth.

summary: Juicy shrimp seared quickly, then finished with lime zest/juice, garlic, and chili for a clean, punchy Citrus-Chili main. Perfect over chili-citrus rice, in bowls, or tucked into tortillas.

yield.notes: Based on ~1.5 lb shrimp. Scale up for bowls or meal prep.

optional.label: Cast iron skillet
optional.notes: Gives an especially nice sear. Stainless works great too.

ingredients.label: Large shrimp, peeled and deveined
ingredients.notes: About 1.5 lb. Tail-on or off is fine. Pat very dry for best sear.

ingredients.label: Kosher salt
ingredients.notes: Plus more to taste at the end.

ingredients.label: Lime zest
ingredients.notes: Zest before juicing; mostly the green part.

ingredients.label: Fresh lime juice
ingredients.notes: About 2 limes. Keep 1 extra lime for wedges if you like.

ingredients.label: Red pepper flakes (or chili powder)
ingredients.notes: Use 1/2 tsp mild, 1 tsp spicy. For chili powder, start at 1 tsp.

ingredients.label: Smoked paprika (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds gentle smoke and deeper color.

ingredients.label: Butter (optional)
ingredients.notes: Adds gloss and rounds the acidity. Use vegan butter or omit.

ingredients.label: Honey (optional)
ingredients.notes: Tiny amount balances lime if your limes are very tart.

steps.title: Dry the shrimp
instructions: Pat shrimp dry with paper towels (this is key for browning).
Add shrimp to a mixing bowl.

steps.title: Quick marinade (8 minutes)
instructions: Add salt, black pepper, lime zest, lime juice, garlic, chili, oil, and optional paprika/cumin to the bowl.
Let sit 8 minutes while you heat the skillet.
checks: Shrimp smells bright and garlicky; evenly coated

steps.title: Sear shrimp in batches
instructions: Heat a skillet over medium-high until hot (a drop of water sizzles instantly).
Add shrimp in a single layer (work in 2 batches if needed to avoid crowding).
Sear 60–90 seconds per side until pink and just cooked through.
Transfer shrimp to a plate as each batch finishes.
checks: Shrimp are opaque and curled into a loose 'C' shape (not tightly curled 'O')
No watery liquid pooling from overcrowding

steps.title: Pan-finish with lime-chili gloss
instructions: If using, add butter and let it melt, scraping up any browned bits.
Add 1–2 tbsp of water if the pan looks dry.
Return shrimp to the skillet and toss for 20–30 seconds to coat.
Optional: stir in honey for balance, then turn off heat.
checks: Shrimp are glossy and coated; lime is bright but not harsh

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Taste and adjust with a pinch of salt or an extra squeeze of lime.
Serve with chili-citrus rice, lemon-chili vegetables, or in tortillas with slaw.

refrigerator.notes: Shrimp are best within 1–2 days; keep airtight.

freezer.notes: Cooked shrimp can be frozen, but texture softens; this recipe is best fresh.

reheat.notes: Reheat gently to avoid rubbery shrimp. Add a splash of water and warm just until hot.

handling: Keep raw shrimp cold until cooking.
Cook shrimp just until opaque; avoid overcooking.

variations.label: Grilled lime-chili shrimp
notes: Thread shrimp on skewers (double-skewer if possible).
Grill over medium-high 1–2 minutes per side.
Finish off-heat with zest + a squeeze of lime and a drizzle of oil or melted butter.

variations.label: Creamy lime-chili shrimp (dairy optional)
notes: After searing, reduce heat and add 2–3 tbsp coconut milk or a spoon of Greek yogurt (off heat) for a creamy coating.
Keep the lime bright; add more zest rather than more juice if needed.

variations.label: Taco/bowl style
notes: Serve in warm tortillas with cabbage slaw and a drizzle of yogurt-lime sauce.
Or serve over chili-citrus rice with avocado and quick pickled onions.

weeknightNotes: Marinate while the pan heats—don’t exceed 15 minutes or lime can start to firm the shrimp.
Cook in batches for true sear and better texture.
